data.title: MoneyMax Financial Services Raises SG$60 Million from Series 14 Tokens; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
MoneyMax Financial Services closed its series 14 of digital securities, raising SG$60.3 million in gross proceeds, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The tokens, bearing an interest rate of 4.50% per annum, were issued to accredited investors and institutional investors. They were listed on the ADDX Exchange on June 13.

Shares of the company were down over 3% in recent trading.

data.title: EDU Holdings' Higher Education Business Posts 118% Surge in Total Student Enrolments; Shares Fall 4%
data.description:
EDU Holdings said total student enrollments for the second trimester of the year in its higher education business, Ikon Institute, surged 118% compared with the same period in the previous year, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Total student enrollments clocked in at 3,725 during the reported period from 1,709 a year earlier, while new student enrollments came in at 711, rising 33% year over year from 533, the filing said.

Shares of the company fell nearly 4% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Acciona Opens Investigation Related To Unit Acciona Construccion
data.description:
Acciona :

  • SAID ON THURSDAY OPENED INVESTIGATION RELATED TO UNIT ACCIONA CONSTRUCCION REGARDING CERTAIN IRREGULARITIES CURRENTLY BEING INVESTIGATED BY SUPREME COURT

  • EXPRESSES ITS "COMPLETE LACK OF KNOWLEDGE, SURPRISE AND CONCERN REGARDING AFOREMENTIONED EVENTS"

  • FORMER EMPLOYEE FERNANDO AGUSTIN MERINO VERA, EXPRESSLY MENTIONED IN AFOREMENTIONED LEGAL PROCEEDINGS, WAS DISMISSED FROM COMPANY ON APRIL 12, 2021

Source text: https://tinyurl.com/3mzvrzpe

data.title: Wall Street Journal - June 13
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Wall Street Journal. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- Peloton Interactive has named Megan Imbres chief marketing officer, making her the fourth marketing leader at the connected fitness company since 2020.

- Warner Bros. Discovery is expected to become one of the first corporate borrowers to restrict creditors from forming certain cooperation groups as the entertainment company completes a debt deal tied to its separation into two public businesses.

- U.S. Senators Bernie Sanders and Angus King introduced a bill Thursday that would ban pharmaceutical manufacturers from using direct-to-consumer advertising, including social media, to promote their products.

- Aerospace startup JetZero plans to start building its blended-wing airplanes in a new Greensboro, North Carolina, factory, slated to begin construction in 2026.

- A federal judge ruled that U.S. President Donald Trump illegally federalised the California National Guard in response to the Los Angeles protests and ordered that the force be returned to the control of Democratic Governor Gavin Newsom.

- Tech and media companies' race to automate advertising poses a threat to the giant agency holding companies that have for decades dominated that work, industry analysts say.

data.title: Jyoti Resins and Adhesives Sets Up Not-for-Profit Arm
data.description:
Jyoti Resins and Adhesives has incorporated a wholly owned subsidiary, Jyoti Social Welfare Foundation, according to a filing with the Indian bourses on Thursday.

The subsidiary has an authorized share capital of 100,000 Indian rupees, with Jyoti Resins holding 100% ownership, the filing said.

The new entity is a not-for-profit company, to be involved in activities including education and coaching, healthcare and medical research, and rural and agricultural development, among others, the company said in the filing.

data.title: Jiangxi Salt Industry's Chairman Resigns
data.description:
Jiangxi Salt Industry Group's chairman, Hu Shiping, resigned due to a job transfer, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

The Chinese salt manufacturer will conduct an election to search for Hu's successor, the filing said.

data.title: Market Chatter: AliExpress Launches Chinese EV Sales in e-Commerce Platform
data.description:
AliExpress has launched direct-to-consumer car sales in the Middle East, becoming the first major Chinese cross-border e-commerce platform to offer full vehicle purchases, YiCai Global reported Thursday.

Models listed in the auto section include BYD's Yuan Plus and Xiaomi's SU7 are now listed in its new auto section, according to the report. Many car brands are taking part in Alibaba's "100 Billion Yuan Subsidy" initiative to boost overseas brand expansion.

The move, to be trialed during this year's 618 shopping festival, marks a shift from business-to-business exports to online business-to-consumer sales, YiCai Global wrote.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Air India flight makes emergency landing in Thailand after bomb threat, Phuket airport says
data.description:
An Air India flight from the Thai island of Phuket headed to Delhi requested an emergency landing on Friday after a bomb threat was received on board, Phuket's airport said,

Flight AI 379 had landed and the airport was proceeding with emergency plans, an official told Reuters.

data.title: Minbos Resources Says Phosphate Rock Fertilizer Trial in Angola Shows Improved Crop Performance; Shares Fall 6%
data.description:
Minbos Resources said that its direct-application phosphate rock fertilizer, Primeiro, demonstrated improved crop performance in the 2024 to 2025 argronomy field trials compared with local practices across several key cropping regions in Angola, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing

Phosphate is widely deficient in Angolan soils, and all field trials have demonstrated a strong crop yield response to it, per the filing.

The firm initiated further replicated trials of a new blended fertilizer including potassium, nitrogen, and sulfur. It plans to expand and refine the fertilizer range in response to farmer feedback and agronomic data.

Its portfolio development program is backed by ongoing local replicated trials and on-farm demonstrations.

Minbos Resources' shares fell nearly 6% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Cyprus ready to assist Middle East evacuations if needed, government says
data.description:
Cyprus has activated a mechanism to assist in the evacuation of third-country nationals from the Middle East region if required, government spokesman Konstantinos Letymbiotis said on Friday.

Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides had called a meeting of the island's National Security Council for later on Friday, he said in a written statement.

data.title: KOP Strikes Off Associated Company
data.description:
Real estate and hospitality firm KOP struck off its indirectly 25% owned associated company, Epic Land, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The striking off is not expected to impact the group's earnings per share for the year ending March 31, 2026.

data.title: Mapletree Logistics Trust Completes Divestment of 8 Tuas View Square in Singapore
data.description:
Mapletree Logistics Trust completed the divestment of 8 Tuas View Square in Singapore, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Following the divestment, the trust's portfolio stands at 178 properties, with assets under management at SG$13.3 billion.

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0537 GMT - The 10-year German Bund yield has continued to trade around the 2.5% level this week, waiting for a new driver, Barclays' rates strategists say in a note. "Markets remain stuck in the waiting room for a catalyst to emerge," they say. This week, various European Central Bank speakers reiterated the message from last week's policy meeting. This implies a need for pause in interest-rate cuts, having reached a neutral policy setting, but not ruling out a further cut later in the year, the strategists say. On Thursday, the 10-year Bund yield closed at 2.481%, down 5 basis points, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0530 GMT - U.S. Treasury yields fall amid renewed geopolitical concerns in the Middle East prompted by Israel's attack on Iran's nuclear facilities. However, market reaction could change direction fast, says Pepperstone's Michael Brown in a note. "Financial markets are always incredibly quick to price in geopolitical fear, but tend to be equally quick to discount it again, seeing the risk premium fade in short order," the senior research strategist says. Treasury yields fall by up to 3 bps across the board; the 10-year yield is down 3 bps at 4.327%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0513 GMT - Israel's strike on Iran has put geopolitical worries back on the agenda, sparking sizeable risk-off moves, but that doesn't mean market participants should tear up long-term views, says Pepperstone's Michael Brown. While the situation remains highly fluid, he cautions that this market reaction has played out numerous times before. "Financial markets are always incredibly quick to price in geopolitical fear, but tend to be equally quick to discount it again." The bull case for equities remains intact, the senior research strategist reckons. The current weakness offers yet another dip-buying opportunity. Brown continues to be a fan of gold, which keeps proving it deserves a place in portfolios as a hedge against uncertainty, adding: "I'd not be touching crude with a bargepole for the time being." (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

"Markets remain stuck in the waiting room for a catalyst to emerge," they say. This week, various European Central Bank speakers reiterated the message from last week's policy meeting. This implies a need for pause in interest-rate cuts, having reached a neutral policy setting, but not ruling out a further cut later in the year, the strategists say. data.title: LEG Immobilien Amends Conversion Price for EUR400 Million Bond
data.description:
LEG Immobilien amended the conversion price of its 400 million-euro bond due Sept. 1 following dividend payments, according to a Friday filing.

The German property company set the new conversion price at 112.9577 euros, compared with the previous 113.2516 euros. The revised conversion price took effect Thursday.

data.title: AP (Thailand) Target Price Lowered to THB8.00 From THB9.50 by Thanachart Securities >AP.TH
Australia's Cettire plunges to record low on US tariff woes, weak demand
data.description:
** Shares of Cettire plunges as much as 21.9% to a record low of A$0.25

** Stock last down 19.5% at A$0.2575, extending losses from Thursday when it flagged continued challenges globally, amplified by U.S. tariff woes, weak demand in April-May season

** About 30 mln shares traded, 3.7x the 30-day avg

** Stock loses 82.7% YTD

data.title: Market Chatter: Foxconn Exports 97% India-Made iPhones to US to Bypass Trump Tariffs
data.description:
Hon Hai Precision Industry, or Foxconn, shipped almost all iPhones from India between March and May to the US to avoid Trump's tariffs on China-made products, Reuters reported Friday, citing customs data.

The Taiwanese company exported iPhones worth $3.2 billion from India during the three-month period, with 97% going to the US, including a near-record $1 billion in May alone, the report said.

This marks a sharp increase from the 2024 average of 50%, showing Apple's move to use India shipments to almost exclusively cater US market, the news wire reported.

Foxconn has already exported $4.4 billion worth of iPhones to the US this year, surpassing the full-year 2024 figure, Reuters wrote.

Neither Apple nor Foxconn responded to Reuters' requests for comment.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Australia's Renascor soars on government nod for battery material facility
data.description:
** Renascor Resources soars as much as 17% to A$0.069, highest since May 26

** Stock last up 13.6%; set for best day in a month

** Minerals explorer receives approval for battery anode material facility from South Australia's Minister for Planning

** About 7 mln shares traded vs 30-day avg of 2.4 mln

** Stock gains ~8% YTD

data.title: Ria Money Transfer's New Approval Enables Digital Salary Payments for Malaysia's Migrant Workforce
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 13, 2025 - (ACN Newswire) - Ria Money Transfer ("Ria"), a global leader in the cross-border money transfer industry and business segment of Euronet Worldwide, Inc. (NASDAQ: EEFT) has been officially approved by the Department of Labour Peninsular Malaysia (JTKSM) for the disbursement of digital wage payments via the Ria Wallet. By streamlining wage distribution through digital means, the Ria Prepaid Card Program, in collaboration with Mastercard, provides an omni-channel solution to employers who may struggle with conventional banking access for their workforce.

The Ria Wallet facilitates various financial activities such as depositing funds, withdrawing cash from ATMs, paying bills, and conducting domestic and international transfers. Customers will also be able to make payments in millions of locations in Malaysia via QR payment linked to DuitNow. In addition, the Ria Wallet streamlines payroll management for Malaysia-based companies throughout industries heavily dependent on migrant workers, including construction, agriculture, and manufacturing.

According to the International Organization of Migration ("IOM"), migrants represent an estimated 15.3% of Malaysia's total workforce, many of whom are unbanked. The Ria Wallet addresses the challenges faced by both workers and employers in the country's cash-centric payment landscape.

Ria's presence in Malaysia since 2001 has positioned Ria as one of the leading players in the money transfer industry. Leveraging its strong physical network of 624,000 locations worldwide and its presence in nearly 200 countries and territories, Ria aims to provide a diverse and seamless service experience to all customers.

Espen Kristensen, Ria Money Transfer's Managing Director Asia Pacific, expressed his excitement for the expansion of Ria's service portfolio in Malaysia. He emphasized, "We understand the challenges employers in Malaysia face when it comes to managing wages for their workers. Through the Ria Wallet, companies will be able to benefit from a digital solution that improves transparency and compliance across their operations."

In collaboration with Mastercard, The Ria Prepaid Card Program underscores the company's commitment to driving positive social impact through the financial empowerment of different communities worldwide.

About Ria Money Transfer

Ria Money Transfer, a business segment of Euronet (NASDAQ: EEFT), delivers innovative financial services including fast, secure, and affordable global money transfers. With the world's largest cross border real-time money movement network, Ria moves money where it matters.

Bridging the gap between digital and physical spaces, Ria's omnichannel products and services provide unprecedented consumer choice, including real-time payments, mobile wallets, currency exchange, home delivery, and cardless ATM payouts. Ria's global infrastructure, powered by the Dandelion real-time, cross-border payments network, facilitates financial access to customers, agents and partners alike. By creating new market opportunities and promoting economic growth around the world, Ria opens ways for a better everyday life.

Source: Ria Money Transfer

Copyright 2025 ACN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: New Zealand Stocks Fall on Mideast Tensions
data.description:
New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index fell 0.8% to close at 12,553 on Friday, snapping a three-day winning streak as escalating tensions in the Middle East weighed on investor sentiment.

Israel conducted airstrikes targeting Iran’s nuclear sites, raising fears of a broader regional conflict and dampening risk appetite across global markets.

Meanwhile, investors monitored trade discussions between the US and China, after both countries agreed to a framework that kept the prospects of a deal on track, but details were yet to be determined.

In domestic news, New Zealand’s manufacturing activity slipped back into contraction in May after four straight months of growth, weighed down by weak demand, rising costs, and waning business confidence amid global economic uncertainties.

Losses were broad-based, with notable declines seen from Mainfreight (-2.6%), Mercury NZ (-1.1%), a2 Milk (-1.1%), Spark NZ (-1.5%), and Fletcher Building (-2.4%).

For the week, the NZX 50 edged down 0.1%.

data.title: India central bank likely steps in after rupee buckles under oil shock
data.description:
A surge in oil prices after Israel’s strikes on Iran sent the Indian rupee tumbling on Friday, prompting the central bank to step in and limit further losses, traders said.

The attack sparked a wave of risk aversion across markets. Asian currencies and equities dropped, U.S. equity futures fell, while safe havens rose. Brent crude surged by as much as 11% at one point.

In response, the rupee slipped to 86.20 per dollar, its weakest level in two months. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) likely intervened to curb volatility and support the currency. A trader told Reuters the central bank likely sold U.S. dollars via state-run banks, stabilising the rupee around the 86.05 level.

The RBI typically leans against sharp moves in the spot market, especially those driven by external shocks, traders noted. The rupee was last quoted at 86.12.

"Price action was stretched at the open, and the RBI likely intervened in small size," said an fx dealer at a private bank. "Having said that, you can't expect this dip (in dollar/rupee) to hold for long looking at oil prices."

Higher oil prices are a significant negative for India, which imports about 80-85% of its crude requirements. A sustained rise in crude worsens the trade and current account deficits, and adds upward pressure on domestic inflation. Additionally, higher oil prices increases dollar demand from refiners.

"There is risk of this escalating, though earlier once Israel attacked Iran with the latter retaliating and both claiming success and denying major damage and the tensions faded," said Madhavi Arora, chief economist at Emkay Global Financial Services.

"However in this case, more details are awaited and in the near term oil would be highly volatile."

Meanwhile, forward premiums rose across tenors, reflecting the potential impact of higher oil prices on Indian interest rates. The 1-year USD/INR annualised premium rose 6 basis points to 1.92%.

data.title: PowerCell, Bosch Expand Hydrogen Partnership With Focus on China
data.description:
PowerCell Sweden expanded its long-term partnership with engineering and technology company Robert Bosch to advance the deployment of hydrogen fuel cell systems, mainly in mobility and stationary applications.

As part of the deal, Bosch acquired intellectual property rights worth 6 million euros to adapt the hydrogen electric system company's S3 fuel cell stack for the Chinese automotive market, according to a Thursday release.

PowerCell will also grant Bosch access to selected non-automotive customers in China, targeting sectors such as backup power, construction, and industrial mobility.

data.title: China Resources Land's Contracted Sales Fall 11% in May
data.description:
China Resources Land recorded contracted sales and gross floor area (GFA) of approximately 18.35 billion yuan and 649,000 square meters in May, down 11.4% and 26.6% from a year prior, a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse stated.

Gross contracted sales for the first five months of 2025 totaled about 86.85 billion yuan, down 6.3% year over year, while sold GFA declined 19% to 3.22 million square meters.

Recurring revenue in May rose by 6.2% to roughly 4.09 billion yuan. Of this, rental income from the investment property business was 2.69 billion yuan, up 13% from a year earlier.

For the first five months of 2025, recurring revenue increased by 9.6% to around 20.44 billion yuan, with rental income from the investment property business rising 12.9% to 13.28 billion yuan.

data.title: Dagang NeXchange Withdraws Proposed Name Change
data.description:
Dagang NeXchange decided to withdraw its proposed special resolution regarding a name change, according to a Thursday Malaysian bourse filing.

The change was halted because Malaysia's Companies Commission found the proposed name was too similar to another company's name outside DNeX's group.

data.title: Vimian Group Enters Deal to Buy US-based AllAccem
data.description:
Vimian Group agreed to purchase US-based veterinary dental sealant product provider AllAccem, according to a Friday release.

The transaction is slated to close Friday, with AllAccem to be integrated into the Swedish animal health company's MedTech segment.

Vimian expects the purchase to have a marginal impact on EPS for the current financial year.

data.title: Thai central bank chief sees need for greater inflation target flexibility
data.description:
Thailand's central bank chief said there was a need for greater flexibility and tolerance regarding deviations from inflation targets, citing risks associated with excessive fine-tuning of monetary policy.

Inflation may deviate from targets more often and more persistently even as it is anchored over time, Bank of Thailand Governor Sethaput Suthiwartnarueput said in a speech published by the central bank on Friday.

"The ability of central banks to steer inflation, especially within narrow ranges, becomes more limited," Sethaput said in London on Wednesday, according to the transcript, noting price shocks and relative price trends.

"We need to recognise clearly the risks associated with overly active and excessive fine-tuning of policy," he added.

Thailand's annual headline inflation rate was negative in May, having been below the central bank's target range of 1.0% to 3.0% for the third consecutive month. It averaged 0.48% in the first five months of the year.

In April, the BOT cut its key interest rate by 25 basis points to 1.75%, the lowest level in two years, due to risks from steep U.S. tariffs. The next rate meeting is on June 25.

Inflation targeting should evolve towards increased flexibility, reducing the heavy emphasis on achieving precise numerical targets and allowing monetary policy to keep a sharp focus on the medium term, Sethaput said.

data.title: Red Avenue New Materials Disposes of Stake in Polyimide-Making Subsidiary for 36 Million Yuan
data.description:
Red Avenue New Materials Group plans to offload its 12% holdings in Suzhou Jucui Materials Technology to Suzhou Aikelong Materials for 36 million yuan, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

After the acquisition, the Chinese rubber and plastic chemical additives manufacturer will no longer hold any equity in the polyimide-making entity, the filing said.

The company's shares slid over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Enity Bank Group AB (publ)'s parent company, Enity Holding AB (publ), today announced the listing of its shares on Nasdaq Stockholm and the outcome of the offering
data.description:
Enity Bank Group

NOT FOR PUBLICATION, RELEASE OR DISTRIBUTION, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY, IN OR INTO THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA, AUSTRALIA, CANADA, JAPAN, HONG KONG, SOUTH AFRICA OR ANY OTHER JURISDICTION WHERE TO DO SO WOULD BE UNLAWFUL OR REQUIRE REGISTRATION OR ANY OTHER MEASURES.

Trading on Nasdaq Stockholm commences today, 13 June 2025. Complete information regarding the offering and the listing of Enity Holding’s shares on Nasdaq Stockholm is available on Enity’s website (www.enity.com).

Juan Navas, Head of Communications, Enity Bank Group

Important information

This communication shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy, nor shall there be any sale of any securities issued by Enity Bank Group AB (publ) or its parent company Enity Holding AB (publ) in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration, exemption from registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such jurisdiction.

Enity Bank Group is a challenger in the Nordic mortgage market, with a vision to make mortgages accessible to more people – regardless of employment type, financial background or age. Through a portfolio of mortgage banks, Enity offers a modern alternative to traditional banks. Since the group was founded as Bluestep Bostadslån in Stockholm in 2005, its operations have expanded to Norway and Finland, with 60plusbanken added in Sweden and Norwegian Bank2 joining the group in April 2024.

Enity Bank Group is under the supervision of the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority (Finansinspektionen). Additional information about Enity can be found on our corporate website https://enity.com
